rationale: Both papers involve identity-based mechanisms in consumer contexts (the citing paper on ownership-driven identity activation/deactivation; the cited paper on self-construal/cause-identity effects), so the citation could plausibly be conceptual rather than incidental. However, the available match evidence is only a single DOI-linked reference with no in-text citation snippet or reference text, and the empirical domains differ (task performance vs donation/discount promotions). With this sparse evidence, it is not safe to drop as an obvious mention-only citation.
